itinerary.title: Fly Kathmandu → Lukla, trek to Phakding
itinerary.description: Take the thrilling 35-minute mountain flight to Lukla (2,860m), then trek downhill through pine forests along the Dudh Koshi River to Phakding.

itinerary.title: Phakding → Namche Bazaar
itinerary.description: Cross several suspension bridges and climb steeply to Namche Bazaar, the 'gateway to the Himalayas'. First views of Everest on a clear day.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day — Namche Bazaar
itinerary.description: Essential rest day. Hike up to the Everest View Hotel (3,880m) for panoramic views. Visit the Saturday market if timing works. Stay hydrated.

itinerary.title: Namche → Tengboche
itinerary.description: Trek past yak herds and rhododendron forests to the famous Tengboche Monastery with stunning views of Ama Dablam and Everest.

itinerary.title: Tengboche → Dingboche
itinerary.description: Descend to Pangboche then climb to Dingboche through high alpine terrain. Excellent views of Lhotse and Island Peak.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day — Dingboche
itinerary.description: Hike to the ridge above Dingboche (5,000m) for acclimatization. Critical day — do not skip. Watch for headache, nausea, dizziness.

itinerary.title: Dingboche → Lobuche
itinerary.description: Trek across the Khumbu glacier moraine to Lobuche. Altitude effects become more pronounced here. Move slowly and stay hydrated.

itinerary.title: Lobuche → Gorak Shep → EBC
itinerary.description: Trek to Gorak Shep (5,164m) for lunch, then push on to Everest Base Camp (5,364m). Emotional and unforgettable. Return to Gorak Shep to sleep.

itinerary.title: Kala Patthar sunrise → Pheriche
itinerary.description: Pre-dawn climb to Kala Patthar (5,545m) for the most iconic Everest sunrise view. Descend all the way to Pheriche for better sleep altitude.

itinerary.title: Pheriche → Namche Bazaar
itinerary.description: Long but rewarding descent. Legs will feel it. Stop at Tengboche for tea and a final view of the Everest massif.

itinerary.title: Namche → Lukla
itinerary.description: Final push back to Lukla. Celebrate with dinner and local Tongba beer. Book a wake-up call — early flights are common.

itinerary.title: Fly Lukla → Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Morning flight back to Kathmandu. Flights are weather-dependent — build buffer days into your itinerary. Rest, recover, celebrate.

itinerary.title: Drive Kathmandu → Besisahar → Bhulbhule
itinerary.description: Drive 7–8 hours to Besisahar, then short walk to Bhulbhule. The circuit begins here along the Marsyangdi River valley.

itinerary.title: Bhulbhule → Jagat → Chame
itinerary.description: Trek through subtropical forest and waterfalls, gaining altitude steadily. Chame is the administrative headquarters of Manang district.

itinerary.title: Chame → Upper Pisang
itinerary.description: Pass the dramatic curved rock face and enter the rain shadow region. Upper Pisang has better views and less crowded teahouses.

itinerary.title: Pisang → Manang
itinerary.description: Trek the high route via Ghyaru and Ngawal for stunning Annapurna views, or take the valley route. Both arrive at Manang.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day — Manang
itinerary.description: Mandatory rest. Hike to Ice Lake (4,600m) or Gangapurna Lake. Attend the daily AMS briefing at the medical post. Do not skip this day.

itinerary.title: Manang → High Camp / Thorong Phedi
itinerary.description: Short but altitude-intensive day. Stay at High Camp (4,850m) for an easier pass crossing tomorrow. Early dinner and early sleep.

itinerary.title: Cross Thorong La Pass → Muktinath
itinerary.description: The highlight of the circuit. Start by 4–5am to cross Thorong La (5,416m) before afternoon winds. Descend to the sacred Muktinath temple.

itinerary.title: Muktinath → Jomsom
itinerary.description: Descend through the dramatic Kali Gandaki gorge, the world's deepest valley. Strong afternoon winds are normal — start early.

itinerary.title: Jomsom → Tatopani
itinerary.description: Many trekkers take a jeep or flight from Jomsom. If trekking, descend through Marpha (apple brandy stop) and Ghasa to hot springs at Tatopani.

itinerary.title: Tatopani → Ghorepani
itinerary.description: Steep climb up through beautiful rhododendron forest to Ghorepani. Hard on the legs after so many days — take it slow.

itinerary.title: Poon Hill sunrise → Nayapul
itinerary.description: Early morning climb to Poon Hill (3,210m) for the famous Annapurna sunrise panorama. Descend to Nayapul and drive to Pokhara.

itinerary.title: Drive to Pokhara / Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Short drive to Pokhara (1.5 hrs) or onward to Kathmandu (7 hrs). Circuit complete!

itinerary.title: Drive Pokhara → Nayapul → Tikhedhunga
itinerary.description: Drive 1.5 hours from Pokhara to Nayapul, then trek through terraced farmland and the busy market town of Birethanti.

itinerary.title: Tikhedhunga → Ghorepani → Chhomrong
itinerary.description: Long climb up stone steps to Ulleri then Ghorepani. Option to detour via Poon Hill. Descend to Chhomrong, gateway to the Annapurna Sanctuary.

itinerary.title: Chhomrong → Bamboo
itinerary.description: Steep descent then climb into the Modi Khola valley. The trail enters dense bamboo and rhododendron forest. Peaceful and atmospheric.

itinerary.title: Bamboo → Deurali
itinerary.description: Climb through the narrow Annapurna Sanctuary entrance past Himalaya Hotel and Dovan. Watch for avalanche-prone sections above Hinku Cave.

itinerary.title: Deurali → Annapurna Base Camp
itinerary.description: Enter the sacred Annapurna Sanctuary — a glacial amphitheater ringed by 7,000m+ peaks. Annapurna I (8,091m) towers directly above. Unforgettable.

itinerary.title: ABC → Bamboo / Chhomrong
itinerary.description: Early morning views at ABC before descending. The descent feels fast compared to the climb. Aim for Chhomrong or Bamboo.

itinerary.title: Chhomrong → Nayapul → Pokhara
itinerary.description: Final descent back to Nayapul and drive to Pokhara. Hot shower and a lakeside dinner well deserved.

itinerary.title: Drive Kathmandu → Syabrubesi
itinerary.description: Long but scenic drive through the hills north of Kathmandu. Syabrubesi is the starting point of the Langtang trek, near the Tibetan border.

itinerary.title: Syabrubesi → Lama Hotel
itinerary.description: Trek through dense forest along the Langtang Khola river. Great birdwatching. The 2015 earthquake caused significant rockfall — visible throughout.

itinerary.title: Lama Hotel → Langtang Village
itinerary.description: The valley opens up dramatically. Pass rebuilt Ghoda Tabela army post. Langtang Village was destroyed in the 2015 earthquake and beautifully rebuilt.

itinerary.title: Langtang Village → Kyanjin Gompa
itinerary.description: Short day across open yak pastures. Kyanjin Gompa has a famous yak cheese factory — try the local cheese. Acclimatize here before going higher.

itinerary.title: Day hike — Kyanjin Ri or Tsergo Ri
itinerary.description: Hike to Kyanjin Ri (4,773m) for panoramic Langtang Lirung views, or push to Tsergo Ri (4,984m) for even broader views. No camping — return to Kyanjin.

itinerary.title: Kyanjin Gompa → Lama Hotel
itinerary.description: Big descent day. Knees will feel the 1,500m drop. Trekking poles highly recommended. Beautiful forest views on the way down.

itinerary.title: Lama Hotel → Syabrubesi → Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Final descent to Syabrubesi then drive back to Kathmandu. Arrive evening — book a hotel room in advance.

itinerary.title: Drive Kathmandu → Soti Khola
itinerary.description: Long drive to the trailhead at Soti Khola. The road passes through Arughat — last major town before the restricted zone.

itinerary.title: Soti Khola → Machhakhola
itinerary.description: Trek through subtropical forest along the Budhi Gandaki river. Hot and humid at lower elevations. Many suspension bridges.

itinerary.title: Machhakhola → Jagat
itinerary.description: Enter the restricted zone — your RAP is checked here. Trek through dramatic gorges with powerful waterfalls.

itinerary.title: Jagat → Deng
itinerary.description: Pass through Philim village. Tibetan cultural influences begin to appear. Landscape becomes more alpine.

itinerary.title: Deng → Namrung
itinerary.description: Trek through Ghap, passing impressive mani walls and chortens. Enter classic Tibetan highland scenery.

itinerary.title: Namrung → Lho → Samagaun
itinerary.description: First views of Manaslu (8,163m) from Lho village. Arrive at Samagaun, base for Manaslu Base Camp day trips.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization — Samagaun / Manaslu BC
itinerary.description: Day hike to Manaslu Base Camp (4,800m) for acclimatization and close-up views of the world's 8th highest peak.

itinerary.title: Samagaun → Samdo
itinerary.description: Short acclimatization day to Samdo near the Tibetan border. Visit the Tibetan refugee settlement.

itinerary.title: Samdo → Dharamsala (High Camp)
itinerary.description: Climb to the high camp below Larkya La. Very basic accommodation — bring layers. Early dinner and sleep.

itinerary.title: Cross Larkya La Pass → Bimthang
itinerary.description: Most demanding day. Start by 4am to cross Larkya La (5,160m) in safe conditions. Long descent to Bimthang through glacial terrain.

itinerary.title: Bimthang → Tilije
itinerary.description: Descend rapidly through dramatic altitude change. Pine and rhododendron forests return. Tilije has apple orchards.

itinerary.title: Tilije → Dharapani → Drive out
itinerary.description: Reach Dharapani where the Annapurna Circuit road begins. Drive to Besisahar or Kathmandu.

itinerary.title: Fly / drive to Jomsom
itinerary.description: Fly from Pokhara to Jomsom (25 min) or drive 8 hours. Prepare permits. Jomsom checkpoint verifies your RAP and group size.

itinerary.title: Jomsom → Kagbeni (Restricted Zone Entry)
itinerary.description: Enter Upper Mustang at the Kagbeni checkpoint — your RAP is checked and stamped here. The landscape immediately becomes more Tibetan.

itinerary.title: Kagbeni → Chele
itinerary.description: Trek through stark red and ochre cliffs into the ancient kingdom. Wind picks up — afternoons can be very strong. Start early.

itinerary.title: Chele → Syangboche
itinerary.description: Cross high passes with views into Tibet. The landscape is unlike anywhere else in Nepal — almost lunar and extraordinarily beautiful.

itinerary.title: Syangboche → Ghami
itinerary.description: Pass the longest mani wall in Nepal. Ghami has one of the best-preserved monasteries in the region.

itinerary.title: Ghami → Lo Manthang
itinerary.description: Arrive at the legendary walled city of Lo Manthang, the ancient capital of the Mustang Kingdom. The king's palace is at the center.

itinerary.title: Explore Lo Manthang
itinerary.description: Visit the four gompa monasteries, the royal palace, and ancient cave dwellings. Hire a local guide for monastery access. Extraordinary culture.

itinerary.title: Lo Manthang → Ghami (return)
itinerary.description: Begin the return journey. Different trail options available — consider going via Charang for variety.

itinerary.title: Ghami → Kagbeni
itinerary.description: Long descent back through the Kali Gandaki valley. Exit the restricted zone at Kagbeni checkpoint — final permit stamp.

itinerary.title: Kagbeni → Jomsom → Fly out
itinerary.description: Short trek to Jomsom then fly back to Pokhara. Flights are weather dependent — build a buffer day into your schedule.

itinerary.title: Drive Pokhara → Nayapul → Tikhedhunga
itinerary.description: Drive 1.5 hours from Pokhara to Nayapul. Trek through Birethanti where permits are checked, then climb to Tikhedhunga.

itinerary.title: Tikhedhunga → Ulleri → Ghorepani
itinerary.description: Climb the famous 3,000+ stone steps to Ulleri — tough but rewarding. Continue through rhododendron forest to Ghorepani. Book a wake-up call.

itinerary.title: Poon Hill sunrise → Tadapani
itinerary.description: Pre-dawn hike to Poon Hill (3,210m) for the most famous Himalayan sunrise view — Dhaulagiri, Annapurna range, and Machhapuchhre all visible. Trek to Tadapani after breakfast.

itinerary.title: Tadapani → Ghandruk → Nayapul
itinerary.description: Descend through the Gurung village of Ghandruk with its traditional architecture and museum. Continue to Nayapul for the drive back to Pokhara.

itinerary.title: Drive Nayapul → Pokhara
itinerary.description: Short drive back. Shortest major trek in Nepal — perfect for first-timers or those with limited time.

itinerary.title: Drive Kathmandu → Dhunche
itinerary.description: Drive north from Kathmandu through winding mountain roads to Dhunche, the entry point to Langtang National Park. Permits checked here.

itinerary.title: Dhunche → Sing Gompa
itinerary.description: Steep climb through forest. Sing Gompa (Chandanbari) has a famous cheese factory run by monks. Good acclimatization stop.

itinerary.title: Sing Gompa → Gosaikunda Lake
itinerary.description: Climb to the sacred Gosaikunda Lake complex — a Hindu pilgrimage site of great significance. Series of high alpine lakes in a glacial bowl. Stunning.

itinerary.title: Gosaikunda → Laurebina Pass → Ghopte
itinerary.description: Cross Laurebina Pass (4,610m) — one of the highest points on the trail. Descend to Ghopte or Phedi if continuing to Helambu region.

itinerary.title: Return to Dhunche / Drive Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Descend back to Dhunche and drive to Kathmandu. Alternatively, extend to Helambu for a longer circuit trek.
